text.title: css-in-js Failure Modes
text.summary: Five CSS-in-JS antipatterns in 2026 React stacks.

antipattern.description: Importing `@emotion/styled` from inside a Server Component tree.
antipattern.reason: Build fails or FOUC at runtime. Emotion's serializer requires a client runtime that does not exist on the server.
Each render creates a new component class — full re-mount of the subtree, every interaction. Performance regression that is silent until profiled.

antipattern.description: Passing `<Button primary>` where `primary` is forwarded to the DOM.
antipattern.reason: React warns ('Unknown DOM property primary'). Switch to `$primary` to keep the prop in JS.

antipattern.description: Theme is typed `any`; `theme.colour` typos go undetected.
antipattern.reason: Refactors silently break; the type system loses authority over theme references.

antipattern.description: Both styled-components and emotion in the same bundle.
antipattern.reason: Doubled runtime overhead, two cascade systems, conflicting specificity. Pick one.
